slots.problem: Do they give up the position before you attack?

questions.text: When I settle my weight on a pinned partner, they give up the position before I attack
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; observable-event version of 'heavy pressure' — partner concedes without requiring an attack (Appendix B 'intense relaxed pressure' note)

questions.text: In my last 10 transitions between dominant positions, my partner recovered guard before I settled the new position
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; observable-event version of 'flow/connection' — partner interruption rate during transitions (Appendix B 'flow → partner doesn't recover guard mid-transition'); scored inverted — higher frequency is worse (flags: inverted)

slots.problem: Do you frame, breathe, and plan before they attack?

questions.text: When a same-rank partner reaches a dominant position on me, I frame, breathe, and move with a plan before they attack
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; composure under pressure — action-based (frame, breathe, plan) rather than felt quality

slots.what: a shifted base or broken grip
slots.problem: Do you enter before they can reset?

questions.text: When a same-rank partner's base shifts or their grip breaks, I enter my attack before they can reset
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; timing/anticipation — entry on moment of imbalance rather than general 'good timing'

slots.problem: Does their defense put them into your next attack?

questions.text: When my first submission attempt fails, my partner's defense puts them in position for my next attack before they reset
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; chaining — second attack enters from the shell of the first, observable from partner's reaction path

slots.problem: Do you strip the grip or frame before they reach a finishing position?

questions.text: When a same-rank partner begins setting up a submission, I recognize and strip the grip or frame before they reach a finishing position
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; early-stage defense — pre-empt before danger zone (vs mid/late escape which is after the hold is set)

slots.problem: Do you create space or change the angle before you tap?

questions.text: When a same-rank partner has a submission set and is finishing, I create enough space or change the angle to escape before I tap
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; late-stage escape — surviving a near-finish, distinct from early defense

slots.problem: Do you find a passing entry within the first minute?

questions.text: When a partner plays a guard style I have not drilled against, I find a passing entry within the first minute of the round
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; adaptability to unfamiliar guard — time-bounded observable outcome, not 'I can handle anything'

slots.problem: Does your attack rate stay above zero in the first two rounds?

questions.text: Against a partner who is significantly heavier or has a much longer reach, my attack rate does not drop to zero in the first two rounds
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; adaptability to body type — attack-rate proxy for adaptation, concrete and measurable in memory

questions.text: Partners or coaches have told me my pressure makes them feel pinned before I start attacking
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; received-feedback version of pressure quality — external observer's report of the felt experience
